intro: The biggest real split between Apache APISIX and Cortex is lock-in posture: Apache APISIX carries low lock-in (apache oss api gateway), while Cortex carries high lock-in (proprietary idp with per-seat lock-in). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Apache APISIX is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. Cortex is built for data warehouse syncing and is not a fit for simple, small projects. On the scoring side, Apache APISIX rates 5/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency; Cortex rates 3/5 and 2/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Apache APISIX you accept that simple api-to-api integrations, while Cortex comes with the reality that simple api-to-api integrations. Neither side is free. Apache APISIX's headline strength in the data is that data warehouse syncing. Cortex's headline strength is that data warehouse syncing. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: Apache APISIX is for data warehouse syncing; Cortex is for data warehouse syncing; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Apache APISIX if you are data warehouse syncing and can accept low lock-in (apache oss api gateway).
verdict_b: Choose Cortex if you are data warehouse syncing and need high lock-in (proprietary idp with per-seat lock-in).

faq.q: Is Apache APISIX cheaper than Cortex?
faq.a: Apache APISIX uses a freemium model, while Cortex uses a seat model; Apache APISIX has a free tier and Cortex does not. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Apache APISIX to Cortex?
faq.a: Apache APISIX has low lock-in (apache oss api gateway); Cortex has high lock-in (proprietary idp with per-seat lock-in). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Apache APISIX; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, Apache APISIX scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Cortex. That said, DX is subjective; the Cortex score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Cortex a good alternative to Apache APISIX?
faq.a: Cortex is built for data warehouse syncing and explicitly not for simple, small projects, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Apache APISIX for data warehouse syncing, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
